vulnerabilities.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is not set. This header mitigates MIME sniffing attacks.
The X-Frame-Options header is not set. This header mitigates clickjacking attacks.
The website does not have a Content Security Policy (CSP) which can help prevent various types of attacks.

fix.law: Implement and enforce a Content Security Policy (CSP)

vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is not set. This header helps prevent SSL-stripping attacks.
The Referrer-Policy header is not set. This header can control how much referrer information is included in the requests.

flaws.name: Missing HTTP Security Headers
flaws.description: The website is missing essential security headers such as Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Strict-Transport-Security which can help prevent various types of attacks.

fix.recommendation: Implement necessary security headers in the website's response headers to enhance security.

flaws.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
flaws.description: The website is vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting attacks which can lead to unauthorized access to user cookies or sensitive information.

fix.recommendation: Validate and sanitize user input on both client and server-side to prevent XSS attacks.
fix.code: Use proper input validation and output encoding techniques to sanitize user input.

flaws.name: SSL/TLS Implementation Issues
flaws.description: There are potential SSL/TLS implementation issues such as outdated protocols or weak cipher suites that can make the website vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks.

fix.recommendation: Ensure the website uses up-to-date SSL/TLS protocols and strong cipher suites.
fix.code: Upgrade SSL/TLS protocols to TLS 1.2 or higher and enable strong cipher suites.

flaws.name: Information Disclosure
flaws.description: The website may be disclosing sensitive information in error messages, headers, or response bodies, which can aid attackers in gathering information about the site and its users.

fix.recommendation: Ensure error messages and responses do not reveal sensitive information.
fix.code: Implement generic error messages and handle exceptions carefully to avoid information disclosure.

flaws.name: Missing Clickjacking Protection
flaws.description: The website is missing X-Frame-Options header which can help prevent clickjacking attacks by preventing the site from being embedded into iframes on malicious websites.

fix.recommendation: Implement X-Frame-Options header with 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' directive to prevent clickjacking.

vulnerabilities.fix: Implement security headers such as Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and Strict-Transport-Security to protect against various types of attacks like XSS, content sniffing, and man-in-the-middle attacks.
Sanitize and validate user input, encode output, and implement secure coding practices to prevent XSS vulnerabilities. Use frameworks that automatically escape output to prevent XSS attacks.
Implement proper access controls and authorization mechanisms to prevent IDOR vulnerabilities. Validate input from users and always verify permissions before allowing access to any resource.
Regularly update software and libraries to the latest versions to patch known vulnerabilities. Use automated tools to monitor for outdated dependencies and apply security patches promptly.
Use strong SSL/TLS protocols and ciphers, enable Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS), and configure HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) to ensure secure communication between clients and servers.
